name: quri-parts-circuit

metadata.name: quri-parts-circuit
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# QURI Parts Circuit

QURI Parts Circuit is a platform-independent quantum circuit library.

## Documentation

[QURI Parts Documentation](https://quri-parts.qunasys.com)

## Installation

```
pip install quri-parts-circuit
```

## License

Apache License 2.0

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.topology.square_lattice.SquareLattice

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.topology.square_lattice.SquareLattice.get_qubit
documentation: Returns qubit index corresponding to the given coordinate.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.topology.square_lattice.SquareLattice.get_coord
documentation: Returns coordinate corresponding to the given qubit index.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.topology.square_lattice.SquareLattice.is_adjacent
documentation:
Check if the given qubits are directory connected to each other on
the lattice.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.topology.square_lattice.SquareLattice.qubit_path
documentation:
Returns sequence of qubits on the shortest path between the
coordinates of two qubits.

Args:
    qubit_a: Qubit index at the starting point.
    qubit_b: Qubit index at the end point.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.topology.square_lattice.SquareLattice._create_aligned_map
documentation:
Create a {class}`QubitToCoordMap` such that the qubit indices
correspond in order from the origin. The x coordinate changes first.

Args:

: xsize: Size of lattice in x direction.
  ysize: Size of lattice in y direction.

Represents qubits connected in a rectangular lattice with no missing
pieces, and searches for the shortest path between any two qubits.

Args:
    xsize: Size of lattice in x direction.
    ysize: Size of lattice in y direction.
    coord_to_qubit: Mapping from coordinates to qubit indices. If omitted,
        it is automatically assigned from the origin with x changing first.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.topology.square_lattice.SquareLatticeSWAPInsertionTranspiler
documentation:
When the connection of qubits is expressed in {class}`SquareLattice`,
insert SWAPs as appropriate before and after for gates applied between two
qubits that are not connected to each other.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_shift.ParameterShiftsAndCoef
documentation:
A pair of parameter shifts and a coefficient appearing in the parameter
shift rule.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_shift.ShiftedParameters

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_shift.ShiftedParameters.get_derivatives
documentation:
Returns a sequence of {class}`~ShiftedParameters`s corresponding
to derivatives of the original {class}`~ShiftedParameters` with respect
to each input parameter.

When the original instance corresponds to the original parametric circuit with
a linear mapping on $\theta^\text{(in)}_i$, given an arbitrary observable
$\hat{O}$, the returned sequence corresponds to derivatives

$$
\left(     \frac{\partial\langle\hat{O}\rangle}{\partial\theta^\text{(in)}_0},     \ldots,     \frac{\partial\langle\hat{O}\rangle}{\partial\theta^\text{(in)}_{m-1}}, \right).
$$

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_shift.ShiftedParameters.get_shifted_parameters_and_coef
documentation:
Returns shifted parameter values and associated coefficients, which
can be used for evaluation of derivatives, given parameter values where
the derivatives are evaluated.

A set of parameter shifts and associated coefficients obtained as a
result of applying parameter shift rule.

Note:

: The current implementation support only linear mappings between input and
  output parameters (i.e. {class}`~LinearParameterMapping`).

When created without `shifts_with_coef`, it corresponds to the original
parametric circuit (i.e. only one component with no parameter shift and coefficient
= 1.0).
Instances returned by {meth}`get_derivatives` method contains pairs of parameter
shifts and associated coefficients. To evaluate derivative of an expectation value
of an observable, estimate an expectation value of the observable with parameters
obtained by {meth}`get_shifted_parameters_and_coef`, multiply it with the
coefficient, and then sum up all terms.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMapping

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMapping.in_params
documentation: Returns a sequence of input {class}`~Parameter`s.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMapping.out_params
documentation: Returns a sequence of output {class}`~Parameter`s.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMapping.mapper
documentation:
Returns a function that maps an input
{class}`~ParameterValueAssignment` to an output
{class}`~ParameterValueAssignment` (i.e. $f$).

It is expected that the function captures the state of
{class}`~ParameterMapping` at the time that this property is accessed and does
not reflect subsequent changes in the state of the {class}`~ParameterMapping`
instance.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMapping.seq_mapper
documentation:
Returns a function that maps a sequence of input parameter values to
a sequence of output parameter values (i.e. $f$).

It is expected that the function captures the state of
{class}`~ParameterMapping` at the time that this property is accessed and does
not reflect subsequent changes in the state of the {class}`~ParameterMapping`
instance.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMapping.mapping
documentation:
Returns a mapping from output parameters to input parameters or
linear functions of input parameters.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMapping.get_derivatives
documentation:
Returns a sequence of {class}`~ParameterMapping`s of derivatives
of the original {class}`~ParameterMapping` with respect to each input
parameter.

The returned sequence corresponds to

$$
\left(     \frac{\partial f}{\partial \theta^\text{(in)}_0},     \ldots,     \frac{\partial f}{\partial \theta^\text{(in)}_{m-1}} \right),
$$

where $f$ is defined as described in {class}`~ParameterMapping` docstring.

ParameterMapping represents a mapping of a set of parameters to another
set of parameters.

It can be considered as a function mapping $m$ input parameters to $n$
output parameters:

$$
(\theta^\text{(out)}_0, \ldots, \theta^\text{(out)}_{n-1}) = f(\theta^\text{(in)}_0, \ldots, \theta^\text{(in)}_{m-1})
$$

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMappingBase

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.ParameterMappingBase.is_trivial_mapping
documentation:
Returns if the mapping is trivial one-to-one mapping (Identity
function).

An abstract class used as a base for implementing
{class}`ParameterMapping`.

Currently this class only has a general implementation of
{meth}`seq_mapper` method.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.LinearParameterMapping

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter_mapping.LinearParameterMapping.get_derivatives
documentation:
Returns a sequence of {class}`~LinearParameterMapping`s of
derivatives of the original {class}`~LinearParameterMapping` with
respect to each input parameter.

Since the original mapping is linear, the returned mappings only contains
constant terms. For example, for the linear mapping defined in the docstring of
{class}`~LinearParameterMapping`, the returned derivatives are as follows:

$$
\begin{align}     \frac{\partial f}{\partial \theta^\text{(in)}_0} &= (0, 0.3)\\     \frac{\partial f}{\partial \theta^\text{(in)}_1} &= (0.1, 0.4) \end{align}
$$

A {class}`~ParameterMapping` representing a linear (affine)
transformation of parameters.

The mapping is represented as a {class}`Mapping` from {class}`Parameter`s to
{class}`ParameterOrLinearFunction`s. For example, if the mapping is defined as:

$$
\begin{align}     \theta^\text{(out)}_0 &= 0.1\theta^\text{(in)}_1 + 0.2, \\     \theta^\text{(out)}_1 &= 0.3\theta^\text{(in)}_0 + 0.4\theta^\text{(in)}_1, \end{align}
$$

the `mapping` argument should be as the following:

```{highlight} python
```

```python
{
    out_param_0: { in_param_1: 0.1, CONST: 0.2 },
    out_param_1: { in_param_0: 0.3, in_param_1: 0.4 },
}
```

where `in_param_0(1)` and `out_param_0(1)` are input and output
{class}`~Parameter` instances respectively.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.ImmutableLinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit
documentation:
An immutable parametric quantum circuit where parameters of parametric
gates are given by linear functions of circuit parameters.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit.add_parameters
documentation:
Add new parameters for the circuit.

Newly created parameters are returned as a sequence. Before
adding a parametric gate to the circuit, all used parameters
should be added by this method or {meth}`~add_parameter`.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit.add_parameter
documentation:
Add a new parameter for the circuit.

Before adding a parametric gate to the circuit, all used
parameters should be added by this method or
{meth}`~add_parametesr`.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit.add_ParametricRX_gate
documentation: Add a parametric RX gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit.add_ParametricRY_gate
documentation: Add a parametric RY gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit.add_ParametricRZ_gate
documentation: Add a parametric RZ gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit.add_ParametricPauliRotation_gate
documentation: Add a parametric Pauli rotation gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_linear_mapped.LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit.extend
documentation:
Extend the parametric circuit with given gates or a linear mapped
unbound parametric circuit.

If the two linear mapped parametric circuit share the same
parameters, they are treated as the same parameters, in contrast
to the case of {class}`~ParametricQuantumCircuit`.

A mutable parametric quantum circuit where parameters of parametric
gates are given by linear functions of circuit parameters.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction._check_valid_probability
documentation:
Probability argument checker.

Checks whether `x` is a correct value as a probability (i.e.,
greater than or equal to 0 and less than or equal to 1), and if
wrong, raises a ValueError with a message including the variable
`name`.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.BitFlipNoise
documentation:
Single qubit bit flip noise.

Args:
    error_prob: Probability of noise generation.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.PhaseFlipNoise
documentation:
Single qubit phase flip noise.

Args:
    error_prob: Probability of noise generation.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.BitPhaseFlipNoise
documentation:
Single qubit bit and phase flip noise.

Args:
    error_prob: Probability of noise generation.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.DepolarizingNoise
documentation:
Single qubit depolarizing noise.

Args:
    error_prob: Probability of noise generation.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction._check_valid_qubit_indices
documentation:
Check if `qubit_indices` is in the correct form of `qubit_count`
qubit noise.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction._is_aligned_square_matrices
documentation:
Tests that the elements of a given sequence are all square matrices of
the same shape.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.PauliNoise
documentation:
Multi qubit Pauli noise.

In the case of multi qubit noise, qubit_indices should specify the qubits on
which the target gate is acting, in any order, without excess or deficiency.

Args:
    pauli_list: Sequence of series of Pauli ids.
    prob_list: Sequence of probability for each Pauli operations.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.
    eq_tolerance: Allowed error in the total probability over 1.
    name: Give Specified name for the noise.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.GeneralDepolarizingNoise
documentation:
Multi qubit general depolarizing noise.

In the case of multi qubit noise, qubit_indices should specify the qubits on
which the target gate is acting, in any order, without excess or deficiency.

Args:
    error_prob: Probability of noise generation.
    qubit_count: Number of qubits of the noise.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.ProbabilisticNoise
documentation:
Multi qubit probabilistic noise.

This noise is defined by giving matrices representing noise gate operations
and the probability of each operation explicitly.

In the case of multi qubit noise, qubit_indices should specify the qubits on
which the target gate is acting, in any order, without excess or deficiency.

Args:
    gate_matrices: Sequence of matrices representing gate operations.
    prob_list: Sequence of probability for each operation.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.
    eq_tolerance: Allowed error in the total probability over 1.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.KrausNoise
documentation:
Multi qubit Kraus noise.

In the case of multi qubit noise, qubit_indices should specify the qubits on
which the target gate is acting, in any order, without excess or deficiency.

Args:
    kraus_list: Sequence of Kraus operator matrices.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.ResetNoise
documentation:
Single qubit reset noise.

Args:

: p0: Reset probability to $|0\rangle$.
  p1: Reset probability to $|1\rangle$.
  qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
  target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.PhaseDampingNoise
documentation:
Single qubit phase damping noise.

Args:
    phase_damping_rate: Probability of phase damping.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.AmplitudeDampingNoise
documentation:
Single qubit amplitude damping noise.

Args:
    amplitude_damping_rate: Probability of amplitude damping.
    excited_state_population: Excited state population.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.PhaseAmplitudeDampingNoise
documentation:
Single qubit phase and amplitude damping noise.

Args:
    phase_damping_rate: Probability of phase damping.
    amplitude_damping_rate: Probability of amplitude damping.
    excited_state_population: Excited state population.
    qubit_indices: Sequence of target qubit indices.
    target_gates: Sequence of target gate names.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.noise.noise_instruction.ThermalRelaxationNoise
documentation:
Sigle qubit thermal relaxation noise.

Args:

: t1: $T_1$ relaxation time.
  t2: $T_2$ relaxation time.
  gate_time: Gate time.
  excited_state_population: Excited state population.
  qubit_indices: Target qubit indices.
  target_gates: Target gate names.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.clifford_approximation.CliffordApproximationTranspiler
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which replaces the non_clifford gate into sequence of
non-parametric Clifford gates.

If the input gate has angles, this transpiler replaces them with the
closest value in the set $\{\pi n /2| n\in\mathbb{Z}\}$. Then
by using the rotation gate transpilers, it is decomposed into a
sequence of non-parametric Clifford gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.bind_parameters
documentation:
Returns a new circuit with the parameters assigned concrete values.

This method does not modify self but returns a newly created
circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.freeze
documentation:
Returns a "freezed" version of itself.

The "freezed" version is an immutable object and can be reused
safely without copying.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.get_mutable_copy
documentation:
Returns a copy of itself that can be modified.

Use this method when you want to get a new circuit based on an
existing circuit but don't want to modify it.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.primitive_circuit
documentation:
Returns the parametric circuit where each gate has an independent
parameter.

Note that some parametric circuit,
e.g. {class}`LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit`, can have non-trivial
mapping of the parameters. In this "primitive circuit", however,
gate parameters are treated as independent,
even if those in the original circuit depend on the same parameters.
For example, if the parametric circuit is defined as:

$$
\begin{align}     U_1(f(\theta_1, \theta_2)) U_2(g(\theta_1, \theta_2)) \end{align}
$$

the primitive circuit should be as the following:

$$
\begin{align}     U_1(\psi_1) U_2(\psi_2) \end{align}
$$

where U1, U2 are rotation gates and f, g are parameter mappings.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.gates
documentation: Returns the gate sequence of the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.has_trivial_parameter_mapping
documentation:
Returns if the input parameters are used for parametric gates
without any conversions.

Note that some parametric circuit, e.g.
{class}`LinearMappedParametricQuantumCircuit`, can have non-
trivial mapping of the parameters.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.param_mapping
documentation: Returns the parameter mapping of the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.bind_parameters_by_dict
documentation:
Returns a new circuit with the parameters assigned concrete values.

This method does not modify self but returns a newly created
circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.__add__
documentation: Returns a new combined circuit with the given gates added.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit_parametric.ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.__radd__
documentation: Returns a new combined circuit with the given gates added.

Interface protocol for a quantum circuit containing unbound (i.e. not
assigned values) parameters.

For circuit execution, the parameters need to be assigned concrete
values by {meth}`~bind_parameters` method.

documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which decomposes multi-qubit Pauli gates into X, Y,
and Z gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.multi_pauli_decomposer.PauliRotationDecomposeTranspiler
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which decomposes multi-qubit PauliRotation gates into
H, RX, RZ, and CNOT gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.multi_pauli_decomposer.ParametricPauliRotationDecomposeTranspiler
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which decomposes multi-qubit ParametricPauliRotation
gates into H, RX, RZ, CNOT, and ParametricRZ gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.CliffordRZSetTranspiler
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler to transpile a QuntumCircuit into another
QuantumCircuit containing only H, X, Y, Z, SqrtX, SqrtXdag, SqrtY,
SqrtYdag, S, Sdg, RZ, CZ, and CNOT.

Since this transpiler involves fusing rotation gates, converting
rotation gates to named gates with a certain precision, and removing
Identity gates, the action of the circuit before and after the
conversion may not be completely equivalent.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.AdjacentGateFuser
documentation: Abstract base class of adjacent gates fusing transpilers.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.CNOTHCNOTFusingTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that reduces the number of CNOT gates by replacing
partial circuits that satisfy certain conditions with equivalent circuits.

The number of Clifford gates (here H, S, Sdag) and the circuit depth will increase,
but the number of CNOT gates will decrease, which is expected to help reduce the
error rate of the circuit in typical NISQ devices.

Ref:
    Joshua Goings, et al., Molecular Symmetry in VQE: A Dual Approach for
    Trapped-Ion Simulations of Benzene, arXiv:2308.00667v1, pp.3-4, (2023).

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.FuseRotationTranspiler
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which fuses consecutive rotation gates of the same
kind acting on the same qubit.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.NormalizeRotationTranspiler
documentation:
Normalize the parameters of the rotation gates (RX, RY, and RZ) so that
they are in the specified range (0 to 2PI by default).

Args:
    cycle_range: Specify a range of width 2PI in the form of (lower limit,
    upper limit). Lower limit is inclusive and upper limit is exclusive.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.RX2NamedTranspiler
documentation:
Convert RX gate to Identity or X gate if it is equivalent to Identity,
X, SqrtX, or SqrtXdag gate.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.RY2NamedTranspiler
documentation:
Convert RY gate to Identity or Y gate if it is equivalent to Identity,
Y, SqrtY, or SqrtYdag gate.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.RZ2NamedTranspiler
documentation:
Convert RZ gate to Identity, Z, S, Sdag, T, or Tdag gate if it is
equivalent to a sequence of these gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.Rotation2NamedTranspiler
documentation:
Convert rotation gates (RX, RY, and RZ) to Identity, Z, S, Sdag, T, or
Tdag gates if it is equivalent to a sequence of these gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.fuse.ZeroRotationEliminationTranspiler
documentation:
Remove rotation gates (RX, RY, and RZ) with rotation angles smaller than
epsilon.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gate_kind_decomposer.U3ToRXRZTranspiler

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.qubit_remapping.QubitRemappingTranspiler
documentation:
Remap qubits in the circuit with the specified mapping.

The mapping `qubit_mapping` should be specified with "from" qubit
indices as keys and "to" qubit indices as values. For example, if
you want to convert a circuit using qubits 0, 1, 2, 3 by mapping
them as 0 → 4, 1 → 2, 2 → 5, 3 → 0, then the `qubit_mapping`
should be `{0: 4, 1: 2, 2: 5, 3: 0}`. The `qubit_count` of the
converted circuit is determined by the largest destination qubit
index. In the above example, the largest index is 5, so the
converted circuit is for 6 qubits.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.identity_manipulation.IdentityInsertionTranspiler
documentation:
If there are qubits to which any gate has not been applied, Identity
gates are added for those qubits.

The application of this transpiler ensures that every qubit has at
least one gate acting on it.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.identity_manipulation.IdentityEliminationTranspiler
documentation:
Generate a new circuit by removing all Identity gates from the given
circuit.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.unitary_matrix_decomposer.SingleQubitUnitaryMatrix2RYRZTranspiler
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which decomposes single qubit UnitaryMatrix gates
into gate sequences containing RY and RZ gates.

Ref:
    [1]: Tomonori Shirakawa, Hiroshi Ueda, and Seiji Yunoki,
        Automatic quantum circuit encoding of a given arbitrary quantum state,
        arXiv:2112.14524v1, p.22, (2021).
    [2]: Main functions are ported from the source code wrtitten by Sota Morisaki
        in QunaSys intern project.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.unitary_matrix_decomposer.TwoQubitUnitaryMatrixKAKTranspiler
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which decomposes two qubit UnitaryMatrix gates into
gate sequences containing H, S, RX, RY, RZ, and CNOT gates.

Raises:
    ValueError: Depending on the nature of the unitary matrix of the input
        UnitaryMatrix gate, the decomposition may fail and throw an error.

Ref:
    [1]: Tomonori Shirakawa, Hiroshi Ueda, and Seiji Yunoki,
        Automatic quantum circuit encoding of a given arbitrary quantum state,
        arXiv:2112.14524v1, pp.4-5, (2021).
    [2]: Main functions are ported from the source code wrtitten by Sota Morisaki
        in QunaSys intern project.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.CircuitTranspilerProtocol
documentation:
Protocol of callable class that transpiles ImmutableQuantumCircuit to
ImmutableQuantumCircuit.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.SequentialTranspiler
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which applies CircuitTranspilers in sequence.

Args:
    transpilers: Sequence of CircuitTranspilers.

Examples:
    .. highlight:: python
    .. code-block:: python

        transpiler = SequentialTranspiler(
            [
                ATranspiler(),
                BTranspiler(arg..),
                ..
            ]
        )
        circuit = transpiler(circuit)

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.GateDecomposer

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.GateDecomposer.is_target_gate
documentation:
Determine if a given gate is subject to decomposition.

Args:
    gate: Gates in the circuit that are scanned from the front.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.GateDecomposer.decompose
documentation:
Describe the specific decomposition process. Only the target gates
satisfying is_target_gate() method are passed.

Args:
    gate: The gates to be decomposed.

Abstract class that represents CircuitTranspiler, such that target gates
are selected by decision function and each target gate is replaced by a
sequence of multiple gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.GateKindDecomposer

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.GateKindDecomposer.target_gate_names
documentation: Returns the set of gate names to be decomposed.

Abstract class that represents CircuitTranspiler, such that each gate is
identified by its gate name and the target gate is replaced by a sequence
of multiple gates.

Classes inheriting from this class can be used for
ParallelDecomposer.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.ParallelDecomposer
documentation:
CircuitTranspiler, which executes given GateKindDecomposer within a
single loop traversing the gate from the front.

Args:
    decomposers: Sequence of GateKindDecomposer with no duplicate gate types
        to act on.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.ParametricCircuitTranspilerProtocol
documentation:
Protocol of callable class that transpiles ParametricQuantumCircuit to
ParametricQuantumCircuit.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.transpiler.ParametricTranspiler
documentation:
Circuit transpiler for ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol, which wraps
CircuitTranspiler and allows it to be applied to
ParametricQuantumCircuitProtocol.

Each successive QuantumGate sequence in the circuit is transpiled respectively and
the results are concatenated. ParametricQuantumGates and parameter mappings are
replicated intact.

Args:
    transpiler: CircuitTranspiler to be wrapped.

functions.name: quri_parts.circuit.clifford_gate.is_clifford
documentation:
Returns True if the input gate is Clifford, otherwise False. In the case of a
rotation gate, whether it is a Clifford gate or not is determined by whether the
rotation angle is in the Clifford angle set $\{\pi n /2| n\in\mathbb{Z}\}$
or not.
This can be applied only to the gates in the set CLIFFORD_GATE_NAMES and currently
implemented rotation gates: {RX, RY, RZ, U1, U2, U3, PauliRotation}. Since newly
defined gates (e.g. U'(a, b) = RZ(2\*a)RY(a+2b)) may return the wrong result, in
this case this function currently returns a NotImplementedError.

Args:

: gate: A gate to be checked whether Clifford or not.
  rtol: The relative tolerance parameter determines how close the angle of

  > rotation is to the angle in the Clifford angle set.

  atol: The absolute tolerance parameter determines how close the angle of

  : rotation is to the angle in the Clifford angle set.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.CliffordConversionTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that converts Clifford gates in a circuit into the
desired Clifford gate sequences.

Convert the Clifford gates in the circuit into gate sequences containing only the
user-specified Clifford gates. Such conversions are done on a best-effort basis and
may leave non target Clifford gates in the output.

Clifford gates that could not be converted or non-Clifford gates will remain in
place.

Args:
    target_gateset: A Sequence of Clifford gate names to output.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.RZ2RXRYTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that converts RZ gates in a circuit into the gate
sequences containing RX and RY gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.RY2RXRZTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that converts RY gates in a circuit into the gate
sequences containing RX and RZ gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.RX2RYRZTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that converts RX gates in a circuit into the gate
sequences containing RY and RZ gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.RX2RZHTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that converts RX gates in a circuit into the gate
sequences containing RZ and H gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.RY2RZHTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that converts RY gates in a circuit into the gate
sequences containing RZ and H gates.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.IdentityTranspiler
documentation: A CircuitTranspiler returns the same circuit as the input.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.RotationConversionTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that converts rotation gates (RX, RY, and RZ) in a
circuit to each other.

Convert rotation gates in a circuit into the gate sequences containing only
specified kinds of rotation gates and Clifford gates.

If there are 2 permitted rotation gate kinds, the rotation gates are converted
into the rotation gates only; if there is 1 permitted rotation gate kind, the
rotation gates are converted into the rotation gates and Clifford gates.

The more favorable Clifford gates can be indicated. However, the choice of Clifford
gates is made on a best-effort basis. Clifford gate kinds included in the output
is not guaranteed.

Args:
    target_rotation: A Sequence of rotation gate names to output.
    favorable_clifford: A Sequence of Clifford gate names to be prioritesed as an
        output.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.transpile.gateset.GateSetConversionTranspiler
documentation:
A CircuitTranspiler that converts the gate set of a circuit into the
specified one.

Depending on the target gate set and the input circuit, the decomposition may fail
and an exception may be raised.

Args:
    target_gateset: A Sequence of allowed output gate names.

modules.name: quri_parts.circuit.gate
documentation: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
: Parametric quantum gate.
: Not intended for direct use. Every gate is created through factory
: methods. A ParametricQuantumGate object contains information of gate
: name, control qubit, target qubit, and pauli ids.
: Non-parametric quantum gate.
: Not intended for direct use. Every gate is created by factory
: methods. A QuantumGate object contains information of gate name,
: control qubit, target qubit, classical bits, parameters, and pauli
: ids.

modules.name: quri_parts.circuit.parameter
documentation: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
: A class representing parameters in parametric quantum circuits.
: A ``Parameter`` is a placeholder and does not hold a concrete value in it.
: Implementation note: equality of Parameters is evaluated as identity of the objects.
: This means that even if two Parameters have the same name they are not equal if
: they are different objects. To achieve this behavior, it is avoided to 1) inherit
: :class:`~NamedTuple` and 2) define ``__eq__`` method.

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.QuantumCircuitProtocol

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.QuantumCircuitProtocol.qubit_count
documentation: Number of qubits involved in the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.QuantumCircuitProtocol.cbit_count
documentation: Number of classical bits involved in the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.QuantumCircuitProtocol.depth
documentation: Returns circuit depth.

Interface protocol for a quantum circuit.

This interface covers all quantum circuit classes, including:

* Non-parametric circuit, parametric circuit and linearly mapped parametric circuit
* Mutable and immutable circuit classes

classes.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.MutableQuantumCircuitProtocol

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.MutableQuantumCircuitProtocol.add_gate
documentation: Add a (non-parametric) quantum gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.MutableQuantumCircuitProtocol.extend
documentation: Extend the circuit with given gate sequence.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.MutableQuantumCircuitProtocol.add_Identity_gate
documentation: Add an Identity gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.MutableQuantumCircuitProtocol.add_X_gate
documentation: Add an X gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.MutableQuantumCircuitProtocol.add_Y_gate
documentation: Add a Y gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.MutableQuantumCircuitProtocol.add_Z_gate
documentation: Add a Z gate to the circuit.

methods.name: quri_parts.circuit.circuit.MutableQuantumCircuitProtocol.add_H_gate

documentation: Adds measurement gate at selected qubits.

Interface protocol for a mutable quantum circuit.

This interface represents quantum circuits that can be modified by adding
{class}`~QuantumGate`s (non-parametric gates).

Some methods (`add_???_gate`) have implementations using an abstract method
{meth}`~add_gate`.
